heading: Local and regional food aid procurement projects
label: 1726c Local and regional food aid procurement projects
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008, and not as part of the Food for Peace Act which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Administrator
children.label: 1 Administrator
children.text: The term “Administrator” means the Administrator of the Agency for International Development.

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: C
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives.

children.label: A
children.text: is produced in, and procured from, a developing country; and

children.label: B
children.text: at a minimum, meets each nutritional, quality, and labeling standard of the country that receives the agricultural commodity, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: described in ; andsection 1722(d) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to nongovernmental organizations, subject to regulations promulgated or guidelines issued to carry out this section, including United States audit requirements that are applicable to nongovernmental organizations.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In accordance with paragraph (2), the Secretary shall provide grants to, or enter into cooperative agreements with, eligible organizations to carry out field-based projects that consist of local or regional procurements of eligible commodities to respond to food crises and disasters in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Consultation with Administrator
children.label: 2 Consultation with Administrator
children.text: In carrying out the development and implementation of field-based projects under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall consult with the Administrator.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Any eligible commodity that is procured for a field-based project carried out under subsection (b) shall be procured through any approach or methodology that the Secretary considers to be an effective approach or methodology to provide adequate information regarding the manner by which to expedite, to the maximum extent practicable, the provision of food aid to affected populations without significantly increasing commodity costs for low-income consumers who procure commodities sourced from the same markets at which the eligible commodity is procured.

children.heading: Impact on local farmers and countries
children.label: A Impact on local farmers and countries
i
children.text: the recipient country of the eligible commodity; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any country in the region in which the eligible commodity may be procured.

children.label: i
children.text: the resale or transshipment of any eligible commodity procured under this section to any country other than the recipient country; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the use of the eligible commodity for any purpose other than food aid.

children.label: I
children.text: world prices for agricultural commodities; or

children.label: II
children.text: normal patterns of commercial trade with foreign countries.

children.heading: Procurement price
children.label: ii Procurement price
children.text: The procurement of any eligible commodity shall be made at a reasonable market price with respect to the economy of the country in which the eligible commodity is procured, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Field-based project grants or cooperative agreements
children.label: d Field-based project grants or cooperative agreements

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants to, or enter into cooperative agreements with, eligible organizations to carry out field-based projects.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant from, or enter into a cooperative agreement with, the Secretary under this subsection, an eligible organization shall submit to the Secretary an application by such date, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Other applicable requirements
children.label: B Other applicable requirements
children.text: Any other applicable requirement relating to the submission of proposals for consideration shall apply to the submission of an application required under subparagraph (A), as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: I
children.text: food surplus regions;

children.label: II
children.text: food deficit regions (that are carried out using regional procurement methods); and

children.label: III
children.text: multiple geographical regions.

children.label: I
children.text: are located in Africa; and

children.label: II
children.text: procure eligible commodities that are produced in Africa.

children.heading: Development assistance
children.label: B Development assistance
children.text: A portion of the funds provided under this subsection shall be made available for field-based projects that provide development assistance for a period of not less than 1 year.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 1 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $80,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.

children.heading: Preference
children.label: 2 Preference
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary may give a preference to eligible organizations that have, or are working toward, projects under the McGovern-Dole International Food for Education and Child Nutrition Program established under section 1736–1 of this title.o

children.label: i
children.text: local and regional agricultural producers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: markets and consumers, including low-income consumers; and

children.label: B
children.text: implementation time frames and costs.
